articles.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
articles.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

articles.title: Tether's USDT hits 2-year countdown threatening its position on U.S. crypto platforms
articles.description: The GENIUS Act has hit its first anniversary without U.S. regulators yet meeting deadlines to write regulations, though rules will be in full effect by July 2028.

articles.title: Kraken says simpler options can unlock crypto's next derivatives market
articles.description: The Wyoming-based firm launched USD-settled bitcoin and ether options, saying product design, not demand, has held back adoption.

articles.title: US agencies miss GENIUS Act deadline for final stablecoin rules
articles.description: US regulators failed to finalize implementing regulations by the GENIUS Act’s one-year deadline, issuing 10 proposed rules instead.

articles.title: South Korean regulator begins sanctions process against Dunamu: Report
articles.description: The proceedings come as South Korea’s Virtual Asset User Protection Act lacks explicit sanctions provisions for hacking and computer system incidents, leaving the scope of penalties uncertain.

articles.title: Meta 平台當機，Facebook、Instagram 同步受影響

articles.title: Bitcoin’s quantum problem gets a recovery tool, but not for Satoshi’s 1.1 million coins
articles.description: Project Eleven says it has funded a proof that lets a wallet's own key-derivation path stand in as ownership after quantum computers can forge its signatures. It runs in 243 milliseconds on a laptop.

articles.title: Electronic Transactions Association CEO Expecting More Partnerships with Bitcoin Startups
articles.description: Electronic Transactions Association (ETA) CEO, Jason Oxman, indicated that members of his organization might start recognizing Bitcoin’s disruptive potential, suggesting that this might lead to more partnerships between traditional electronic payment providers and Bitcoin startups.

articles.title: Inside Zcash's new node that targets Visa-scale privacy at 50,000 transactions per second
articles.description: The new Zakura client is the first live piece of a plan to take Zcash from roughly one private transaction per second to payment-network scale.
